contentBlocks.original:
प्रातः स्मरामि हृदि संस्फुरदात्मतत्त्वं
सच्चित्सुखं परमहंस-गतिं तुरीयम्।
यत्स्वप्न-जागर-सुषुप्तमवैति नित्यं
तद् ब्रह्म निष्कलमहं न च भूतसङ्घः ॥१॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
prātaḥ smarāmi hṛdi saṃsphuradātmatattvaṃ
saccitsukhaṃ paramahaṃsa-gatiṃ turīyam.
yatsvapna-jāgara-suṣuptamavaiti nityaṃ
tad brahma niṣkalamahaṃ na ca bhūtasaṅghaḥ ..1..
contentBlocks.translation: At dawn I remember the Reality which is the Self, shining brilliantly in the heart, existence-consciousness-happiness, the goal of Paramahamsa sannyasins (sages), the Fourth; That which knows always the states of dream, waking and deep-sleep, that Brahman which is partless I am, not the cluster of elements.
contentBlocks.meanings: प्रातः (prātaḥ) – at dawn; स्मरामि (smarāmi) – I remember; हृदि (hṛdi) – in the heart; संस्फुरदात्मतत्त्वं (saṃsphuradātmatattvaṃ) – shining Self-reality (saṃsphurat:shining | ātma:Self | tattvaṃ:reality); सच्चित्सुखं (saccitsukhaṃ) – existence-consciousness-happiness (sat:existence | cit:consciousness | sukhaṃ:happiness); परमहंस-गतिं (paramahaṃsa-gatiṃ) – goal of Paramahamsa (paramahaṃsa:Paramahamsa | gatiṃ:goal); तुरीयम् (turīyam) – the Fourth; यत् (yat) – which; स्वप्न-जागर-सुषुप्तम् (svapna-jāgara-suṣuptam) – dream, waking, deep-sleep (svapna:dream | jāgara:waking | suṣuptam:deep-sleep); अवैति (avaiti) – knows; नित्यं (nityaṃ) – always; तद् (tad) – that; ब्रह्म (brahma) – Brahman; निष्कलम् (niṣkalam) – partless; अहम् (aham) – I am; न (na) – not; च (ca) – and; भूतसङ्घः (bhūtasaṅghaḥ) – cluster of elements (bhūta:elements | saṅghaḥ:cluster);

contentBlocks.original:
प्रातर्भजामि मनसा वचसामगम्यं
वाचो विभान्ति निखिला यदनुग्रहेण।
यं नेति नेति वचनैर्निगमा अवोचुः
तं देव-देवमजमच्युतमाहुरग्र्यम् ॥२॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
prātarbhajāmi manasā vacasāmagamyaṃ
vāco vibhānti nikhilā yadanugraheṇa.
yaṃ neti neti vacanairnigamā avocuḥ
taṃ deva-devamajamacyutamāhuragryam ..2..
contentBlocks.translation: At dawn I sing the praise of That which is unattainable by mind and speech, but by the grace of which all words shine. That which the scriptures declares through the words `not this', `not this'- That God of gods, they say, is unborn and un-changing.
contentBlocks.meanings: प्रातः (prātaḥ) – at dawn; भजामि (bhajāmi) – I sing the praise; मनसा (manasā) – by mind; वचसा (vacasā) – by speech; अगम्यम् (agamyam) – unattainable; वाचः (vācaḥ) – words; विभान्ति (vibhānti) – shine; निखिलाः (nikhilāḥ) – all; यदनुग्रहेण (yadanugraheṇa) – by whose grace (yat:whose | anugraheṇa:by grace); यम् (yam) – which; नेति (neti) – not this; नेति (neti) – not this; वचनैः (vacanaiḥ) – by words; निगमाः (nigamāḥ) – scriptures; अवोचुः (avocuḥ) – declared; तम् (tam) – that; देव-देवम् (deva-devam) – God of gods (deva:god | devam:god); अजम् (ajam) – unborn; अच्युतम् (acyutam) – unchanging; आहुः (āhuḥ) – they say; अग्र्यम् (agryam) – foremost;

contentBlocks.original:
प्रातर्नमामि तमसः परमर्कवर्णं
पूर्णं सनातनपदं पुरुषोत्तमाख्यम्।
यस्मिन्निदं जगदशेषमशेषमूर्तौ
रज्ज्वां भुजङ्गम इव प्रतिभासितं वै ॥३॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
prātarnamāmi tamasaḥ paramarkavarṇaṃ
pūrṇaṃ sanātanapadaṃ puruṣottamākhyam.
yasminnidaṃ jagadaśeṣamaśeṣamūrtau
rajjvāṃ bhujaṅgama iva pratibhāsitaṃ vai ..3
contentBlocks.translation: At dawn I bow to that which is called the Highest Self which is beyond darkness, of the hue of the Sun the ancient goal which is the plenum - That, the residuless form (i.e. the whole) in which the entire universe is made manifest like a serpent in a rope.
contentBlocks.meanings: प्रातः (prātaḥ) – at dawn; नमामि (namāmi) – I bow; तमसः (tamasaḥ) – of darkness; परम् (param) – beyond; अर्कवर्णम् (arkavarṇam) – of the hue of the Sun (arka:Sun | varṇam:hue); पूर्णम् (pūrṇam) – plenum; सनातनपदम् (sanātanapadam) – ancient goal (sanātana:ancient | padam:goal); पुरुषोत्तमाख्यम् (puruṣottamākhyam) – called the Highest Self (puruṣottama:Highest Self | ākhyam:called); यस्मिन् (yasmin) – in which; इदं (idaṃ) – this; जगत् (jagat) – universe; अशेषम् (aśeṣam) – entire; अशेषमूर्तौ (aśeṣamūrtau) – in the residuless form (aśeṣa:residuless | mūrtau:in the form); रज्ज्वाम् (rajjvām) – in the rope; भुजङ्गमः (bhujaṅgamaḥ) – serpent; इव (iva) – like; प्रतिभासितम् (pratibhāsitam) – made manifest; वै (vai) – indeed;

contentBlocks.original:
श्लोकत्रयमिदं पुण्यं लोकत्रय-विभूषणम्।
प्रातःकाले पठेद्यस्तु स गच्छेत्परमं पदम् ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
ślokatrayamidaṃ puṇyaṃ lokatraya-vibhūṣaṇam.
prātaḥkāle paṭhedyastu sa gacchetparamaṃ padam ..
contentBlocks.translation: This meritorious triad of verses, the ornament of the three words - he who reads at the time of dawn goes to the supreme goals. This is the pahala-sruti (description of the fruit) of this Vedantic prayer. It is an eulogy of the prayer whose purpose is to consecrate the thoughts, words, and deeds of the individual so that the final goal may eventually be gained.
contentBlocks.meanings: श्लोकत्रयम् (ślokatrayam) – triad of verses (śloka:verse | trayam:triad); इदं (idaṃ) – this; पुण्यम् (puṇyam) – meritorious; लोकत्रय-विभूषणम् (lokatraya-vibhūṣaṇam) – ornament of the three worlds (loka:world | traya:three | vibhūṣaṇam:ornament); प्रातःकाले (prātaḥkāle) – at the time of dawn (prātaḥ:dawn | kāle:at the time); पठेत् (paṭhet) – reads; यः (yaḥ) – he who; तु (tu) – indeed; स (sa) – he; गच्छेत् (gacchet) – goes; परमं (paramaṃ) – supreme; पदम् (padam) – goal;
